story_summary: The hollow chapel's clock stops on the longest night; Boxwell and Tobias find a moonstone pendant wedged in the movement, and the terrier Pip leads them to the stone mill.

entities.summary: A patient travelling clockmaker who carries a cedar case of tools.
entities.evidence_excerpt: Boxwell climbed the chapel ladder with a lamp.

entities.summary: Tobias's family mill on the edge of the hollow, its wheel frozen for the winter.
entities.evidence_excerpt: The stone mill stood silent above the iced race.

entities.summary: A pale moonstone pendant found jammed in the chapel clock's movement.
entities.evidence_excerpt: A moonstone pendant had stopped the escapement dead.

entities.summary: The little chapel whose ancient clock governs the hollow's days.
entities.evidence_excerpt: The hollow chapel's clock had not missed an hour in a century.

entities.summary: Tobias's quick brown terrier, with a nose for hidden things.
entities.evidence_excerpt: Pip scratched at the mill door until they followed.

claims.evidence_excerpt: The chapel stands at the heart of the hollow.
the cedar case never left his side
Tobias's family had kept the stone mill for generations.

chunks.title: The chapel clock mystery
chunks.body: The hollow chapel's clock stopped on the longest night because a moonstone pendant was wedged in its movement — no one will say whose it was.

chunks.title: Continuity: The Chapel Clock
chunks.body: Previous story summary: Boxwell and Tobias freed the chapel clock of a moonstone pendant, and Pip the terrier led them to the frozen stone mill.

open_questions: Whose moonstone pendant stopped the chapel clock, and how did it get there?
